VOWELS = Set("aeiou")

def solve(s : String) : String
  # Step 1 & 2: Calculate frequencies and first indices
  counts = Hash(Char, Int).new
  first_index = Hash(Char, Int).new
  unique_vowels = Set(Char).new

  # First pass to collect counts and unique vowels
  s.each_char do |char|
    if VOWELS.include?(char)
      counts[char] = (counts[char] || 0) + 1
      unique_vowels.add(char)
    end
  end

  # Second pass to determine the first appearance index for each unique vowel
  unique_vowels.each do |char|
    s.each_char.with_index do |c, i|
      if c == char
        first_index[char] = i
        break
      end
    end
  end
  
  # Step 3: Get sorted list of unique vowels
  sorted_vowels = unique_vowels.to_a.sort do |v1, v2|
    c1 = counts[v1]
    c2 = counts[v2]
    i1 = first_index[v1]
    i2 = first_index[v2]

    # Primary sort: Frequency non-increasing (descending)
    if c1 != c2
      # c2 <=> c1 sorts descendingly by frequency
      c2 <=> c1
    else
      # Secondary sort: First appearance position (ascending)
      i1 <=> i2
    end
  end

  # Step 4: Construct the target sequence of vowels
  vowel_sequence = Array(Char).new
  sorted_vowels.each do |vowel|
    count = counts[vowel]
    count.times do
      vowel_sequence << vowel
    end
  end
  
  # Step 5: Reconstruction
  result_chars = Array(Char).new(s.length)
  vowel_ptr = 0
  
  s.each_char.with_index do |char, i|
    if VOWELS.include?(char)
      # This character is a vowel, replace it with the next sorted vowel
      result_chars[i] = vowel_sequence[vowel_ptr]
      vowel_ptr += 1
    else
      # Consonant, keep it
      result_chars[i] = char
    end
  end
  
  return result_chars.join
end

  counts = Hash(Char, Int)
  first_index = Hash(Char, Int)
  unique_vowels = Set(Char)

    count.times do
      vowel_sequence << vowel
    end

  result_chars = Array(Char).new(s.length)

      vowel_ptr += 1

    end
  end
  
  return result_chars.join
end

require "collections"

def solve(s : String) : String
  # Helper function to check if a character is a vowel
  def is_vowel?(c : Char) : Bool
    c in %["a", "e", "i", "o", "u"]
  end

  # 1. Preprocessing: Calculate Frequencies and First Indices
  freqs = Hash(Char, Int)
  first_indices = Hash(Char, Int)
  unique_vowels = Set(Char)

  s.each_char.with_index do |c, i|
    if is_vowel?(c)
      unique_vowels.add(c)
      freqs[c] = (freqs[c] ?? 0) + 1
      
      # Only set the first index if it hasn't been set yet
      if !first_indices.key?(c)
        first_indices[c] = i
      end
    end
  end

  # Convert unique vowels set to an array for sorting
  vowels_list = unique_vowels.to_a

  # 2. Sorting the Vowels
  # We sort the unique vowels based on the required criteria.
  # Comparator logic:
  # 1. Frequency (descending)
  # 2. First appearance index (ascending)
  
  # Crystal's sort method uses a block that should return true if element A comes before element B.
  sorted_vowels = vowels_list.sort do |v1, v2|
    freq1 = freqs[v1]
    freq2 = freqs[v2]
    
    # Primary criterion: Frequency (Descending)
    if freq1 != freq2
      # v1 comes before v2 if freq1 > freq2
      freq1 > freq2
    else
      # Secondary criterion: First Index (Ascending)
      # v1 comes before v2 if index1 < index2
      first_indices[v1] < first_indices[v2]
    end
  end

  # 3. Construct the Target Vowel Sequence
  target_vowel_sequence = Array(Char)
  sorted_vowels.each do |v|
    count = freqs[v]
    count.times do
      target_vowel_sequence.push(v)
    end
  end

  # 4. Reconstruction
  result_chars = Array(Char).new(s.length)
  vowel_idx = 0 # Pointer for target_vowel_sequence

  s.each_char.with_index do |c, i|
    if is_vowel?(c)
      # Replace vowel with the next vowel from the sorted sequence
      result_chars[i] = target_vowel_sequence[vowel_idx]
      vowel_idx += 1
    else
      # Consonant remains in place
      result_chars[i] = c
    end
  end

  result_chars.join
end

def is_vowel?(char : Char) : Bool
  char == 'a' || char == 'e' || char == 'i' || char == 'o' || char == 'u'
end

def solve(s : String) : String
  
  # 1. Collect data
  vowel_indices = Array(Int32).new(0)
  vowel_counts = Hash(String, Int32).new
  first_appearance_index = Hash(String, Int32).new
  
  # Iterate through s to collect necessary metadata
  s.each_char.with_index do |char, index|
    if is_vowel?(char)
      vowel_indices.push(index)
      
      vowel_str = char.to_string
      
      # Update counts
      vowel_counts[vowel_str] ||= 0
      vowel_counts[vowel_str] += 1
      
      # Update first appearance index
      if !first_appearance_index.key?(vowel_str)
        first_appearance_index[vowel_str] = index
      end
    end
  end
  
  # 2. Determine sorted vowel order
  
  # Get distinct vowels (as Chars)
  distinct_vowels = vowel_counts.keys.map { |k| k[0] }.to_a
  
  # Sort the distinct vowels based on frequency (desc) then first appearance (asc)
  distinct_vowels.sort! do |v1, v2|
    freq1 = vowel_counts[v1.to_string]
    freq2 = vowel_counts[v2.to_string]
    
    # Primary key: Frequency (descending)
    if freq1 != freq2
      # freq2 <=> freq1 achieves descending order (e.g., 3 vs 1 -> 1 <=> 3 is -2)
      freq2 <=> freq1 
    else
      # Secondary key: First appearance index (ascending)
      idx1 = first_appearance_index[v1.to_string]
      idx2 = first_appearance_index[v2.to_string]
      idx1 <=> idx2
    end
  end
  
  # 3. Build the sequence of vowels to insert
  
  sorted_vowel_sequence = Array(Char).new
  
  distinct_vowels.each do |v|
    count = vowel_counts[v.to_string]
    count.times do
      sorted_vowel_sequence.push(v)
    end
  end
  
  # 4. Construct the resulting string
  
  # Convert s to mutable array of chars for modification
  result_chars = s.chars.to_a
  
  # Replace vowels at their original positions
  vowel_indices.each_with_index do |index, seq_index|
    result_chars[index] = sorted_vowel_sequence[seq_index]
  end
  
  return result_chars.join
end

```crystal
require "string"

def is_vowel?(char : Char) : Bool
  char == 'a' || char == 'e' || char == 'i' || char == 'o' || char == 'u'
end

def solve(s : String) : String
  
  # 1. Collect data
  vowel_indices = Array(Int32).new(0)
  vowel_counts = Hash(String, Int32).new
  first_appearance_index = Hash(String, Int32).new
  
  # Iterate through s to collect necessary metadata
  s.each_char.with_index do |char, index|
    if is_vowel?(char)
      vowel_indices.push(index)
      
      # Update counts
      vowel_counts[char.to_string] ||= 0
      vowel_counts[char.to_string] += 1
      
      # Update first appearance index
      if !first_appearance_index.key?(char.to_string)
        first_appearance_index[char.to_string] = index
      end
    end
  end
  
  # 2. Determine sorted vowel order
  
  # Get distinct vowels
  distinct_vowels = vowel_counts.keys.map { |k| k[0] }.to_a
  
  # Sort the distinct vowels based on frequency (desc) then first appearance (asc)
  distinct_vowels.sort! do |v1, v2|
    freq1 = vowel_counts[v1.to_string]
    freq2 = vowel_counts[v2.to_string]
    
    # Primary key: Frequency (descending)
    if freq1 != freq2
      freq2 <=> freq1 # Comparison: 2 <=> 1 is 1 (v1 comes after v2 if freq1 < freq2)
    else
      # Secondary key: First appearance index (ascending)
      idx1 = first_appearance_index[v1.to_string]
      idx2 = first_appearance_index[v2.to_string]
      idx1 <=> idx2
    end
  end
  
  # 3. Build the sequence of vowels to insert
  
  sorted_vowel_sequence = Array(Char).new
  
  distinct_vowels.each do |v|
    count = vowel_counts[v.to_string]
    count.times do
      sorted_vowel_sequence.push(v)
    end
  end
  
  # 4. Construct the resulting string
  
  # Convert s to mutable array of chars for modification
  result_chars = s.chars.to_a
  
  # Replace vowels at their original positions
  vowel_indices.each_with_index do |index, seq_index|
    result_chars[index] = sorted_vowel_sequence[seq_index]
  end
  
  return result_chars.join
end
```
